description: Converge the sequential and parallel/wave loop-engine execution paths by relocating all non-path-specific main-thread post-Claude reactions into a single src/loop_engine/reactions/ module that both paths route through, enforced by #[deprecated] + #![deny(deprecated)] on iteration.rs/wave_scheduler.rs/slot.rs and exhaustive param-struct destructure on the five coordinators. Fixes bug #3 (wave mode never waits on rate/session limits, false-aborting and resetting in-flight work). Minimum bar: converge AND lock six audit items — #2 crash escalation (+effort), #3 pre-iteration usage gate, #5 overflow recovery, #6 rate-limit wait, #10 human-review, #13 budget accounting. Authoritative spec: ~/.claude/plans/what-can-we-do-breezy-shore.md + tasks/grok-review-convergence-plan.md + tasks/grok-review-spike-enforcement.md (SPIKE-001).

criteria: Rust: no warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
Rust: scoped tests pass (`cargo test -p task-mgr <module>`); full suite green at milestones
No breaking changes to public APIs unless the task explicitly relocates a leaf (then a transition #[deprecated] shim + #[allow(deprecated)] at legit sites)
Each of the five reactions:: coordinators destructures its param struct exhaustively (no `..`)
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. This is an all-or-nothing platform refactor: the reactions module, enforcement, harness, and the six converged behaviors land together. Squash optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Single home: every converged reaction lives in reactions:: and is called by both paths; never copy-paste into one path
Wave folds N: the wave path applies a reaction over its N slot results; sequential over its 1 result
Account-global reactions (rate-limit) fire exactly ONCE per wave
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at milestones — milestones must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Relocate behind unchanged signatures first, move call sites second, delete shims last (FR-CLEANUP) — keep recovery.rs/overflow.rs tests green throughout

prohibitedOutcomes: Copy-pasting a reaction into one path instead of calling the shared coordinator
A rate-limit early return that zeroes ctx.consecutive_merge_fail_waves (wipes the cascade-halt defense)
Resetting a task to todo that completed in the same wave (must filter on status='in_progress')
N sequential usage waits for N rate-limited slots — the wait must fire exactly once
Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check a type without verifying content
A coordinator param struct destructured with `..` (defeats the compile-time parity lock)
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context; unwrap() on fallible paths in production

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Consumer Impact Table generated in progress log covering all callers of the six leaf functions
Every engine.rs `pub use` re-export of a relocated leaf is identified with its external-test consumers (FR-008)
slot.rs:492 handle_prompt_too_long caller confirmed (routes through reactions::post_output::handle_overflow per FR-OQ)
Test files calling relocated leaves listed with the repoint-or-#[allow(deprecated)] decision
Deny-scope confirmed as iteration.rs + wave_scheduler.rs + slot.rs

Produce a Consumer Impact Table in the progress log: file:line, which leaf, OK / BREAKS / NEEDS_REVIEW, and the migration (route through coordinator vs #[allow(deprecated)] at a legit transition site).

Distinguish production callers (route through coordinator) from legit transition sites (engine re-exports, tests → #[allow(deprecated)])

acceptanceCriteria: src/loop_engine/reactions/{mod,pre_spawn,account,post_output,post_completion}.rs exist and are registered in loop_engine/mod.rs
Five coordinator fns declared: resolve_task_execution, account_usage_gate, react_to_outputs, handle_overflow, react_to_completions — each destructures its param struct with NO `..`
#![deny(deprecated)] is active at the top of iteration.rs, wave_scheduler.rs, AND slot.rs

At least one to-be-relocated leaf carries #[deprecated]; demonstrate (in the progress log) that calling it from any of the three engine files fails `cargo build` (then route through the coordinator or #[allow] at a legit site)
cargo check + cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean; existing recovery.rs/overflow.rs/iteration_pipeline_parity.rs tests still green

Note in progress log: the parity-harness skeleton (tests/reaction_parity.rs) is created by TEST-INIT-001; the CLAUDE.md 'Reaction framework (shared)' section + the iteration_pipeline.rs 'Out of scope' rate-limit removal are owned by VERIFY-001 (the authoritative doc gate)
userStories.description: Create src/loop_engine/reactions/{mod,pre_spawn,account,post_output,post_completion}.rs registered in loop_engine/mod.rs, with the five coordinator entry points (signatures stubbed/typed but bodies may delegate to the still-in-place leaves initially). Establish the SPIKE-001 enforcement mechanism: add #![deny(deprecated)] to iteration.rs + wave_scheduler.rs + slot.rs, and mark the to-be-relocated leaves #[deprecated]. (The tests/reaction_parity.rs harness skeleton is owned by TEST-INIT-001; the CLAUDE.md 'Reaction framework (shared)' section + the iteration_pipeline.rs 'Out of scope' rate-limit removal are owned by VERIFY-001 — do NOT create them here, to keep this contract's blast radius bounded.) This contract gates all six FEATs.
userStories.notes: FR-OQ RESOLVED: slot.rs routes through handle_overflow and carries the deny. Exhaustive-destructure precedent: ProcessingParams in iteration_pipeline.rs / tests/iteration_pipeline_parity.rs:1196-1322. Deprecation precedent: recovery.rs:428, slot.rs:336. Bodies may temporarily delegate to in-place leaves; FEAT-002/003/005/006/010/013 fill them in. Learning #2300/#2157: parity tests run identical fixtures through both path shapes.
qualityDimensions: The compile-time lock is the primary anti-drift guarantee: re-inlining a relocated leaf into the three engine files MUST fail the build
Coordinator param structs use exhaustive destructure (no `..`) so adding a field breaks compilation at both call sites
Keep the deny narrowly scoped to the three engine files; legit transition sites (engine re-exports, tests) use targeted #[allow(deprecated)]
Idiomatic: pub(crate) surface inside reactions::; thin coordinators
userStories.title: reactions:: module boundary + enforcement mechanism + parity-harness skeleton

acceptanceCriteria: Wait-once: a 3-slot item-slice with 2 RateLimit items fires the injected wait closure EXACTLY once
Mixed-wave durability: a slice with one RateLimit + one Completed leaves the completed task `done` and resets only the rate-limited task to `todo`
RateLimit-output inert: a RateLimit item's output produces zero learnings/completions/status-updates (assert DB state)
Parse-fail fallback: unparseable reset output → wait closure invoked with the fallback_wait value (both shapes agree)
Negative control: a slice with no RateLimit returns AccountReaction::None and performs ZERO DB writes
Stop: injected stop/signal during the wait → AccountReaction::Stop
Known-bad discriminator: a naive 'loop over items calling wait per item' stub FAILS the wait-once test
Fixtures use production-shaped structs (OutputReactionItem, real task rows), not hand-built maps
Establishes the tests/reaction_parity.rs harness skeleton (two independent DBs, TASK_MGR_NO_EXTRACT_LEARNINGS=1, public-API setup helpers) reused by TEST-INIT-002/003/004
userStories.description: Write failing/ignored tests in tests/reaction_parity.rs for reactions::account::react_to_outputs covering the rate-limit (#6) behavior across the sequential (1-item) and wave (N-item, incl. mixed) shapes. Inject the wait as a counting closure (react_to_outputs_inner) so tests are hermetic (no OAuth / usage API / real sleep).
edgeCases: Mixed wave: 1 completed + 1 rate-limited → completed stays done
RateLimit message as output → zero learnings/completions/status-updates
Unparseable reset time → fallback_wait used identically on both shapes
No RateLimit in slice → AccountReaction::None, zero DB writes
Rate-limit wave while merge-fail streak=1 → streak preserved, NOT zeroed (B3): a merge-fail→rate-limit→merge-fail sequence reaches the halt threshold

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Single wait, all in-progress tasks reset to todo, WaitedAndRetry
Returns Stop; caller emits terminal exit 130

invariants: react_to_outputs performs at most one wait per call regardless of N
Only status='in_progress' rows are reset; 'done' rows are never reverted
userStories.notes: Harness conventions: two independent DBs, TASK_MGR_NO_EXTRACT_LEARNINGS=1, public-API setup (per tests/iteration_pipeline_parity.rs header). Inner/outer split mirrors maybe_fire/maybe_fire_inner (auto_review.rs).
qualityDimensions: Wait-once is the headline invariant — the test must reject a per-item wait stub
Mixed-wave durability pins the B1 ordering (completion before reset)
userStories.title: TDD: rate-limit account reaction (react_to_outputs) parity

acceptanceCriteria: resolve_task_execution returns an identical TaskExecutionPlan (model, effort, runner) for the same (ctx, task, prompt_result, config) regardless of path shape
Effort override flows through (a prior overflow effort_override is reflected in the plan) — guards the #6-effort requirement
Crash escalation: a task flagged crashed escalates model on re-pick; non-crash does not
Override invalidation: an out-of-band tasks.model edit clears the six per-task recovery channels (operator escape valve preserved)
account_usage_gate returns the same GateDecision for the same usage state on both shapes
Known-bad discriminator: a plan that drops effort_overrides FAILS the effort test
userStories.description: Failing/ignored parity tests for reactions::pre_spawn::resolve_task_execution (crash escalation + model AND effort overrides + override invalidation + review routing) and account_usage_gate, asserting identical results for sequential (1 call) and wave (per-slot) shapes.
edgeCases: Prior overflow left an effort_override → plan reflects it
Crashed-last task re-picked → escalates; clean task → no escalation
Out-of-band tasks.model edit → recovery channels cleared
review-class task → reviewModel routing applied after escalation

failureModes.expectedBehavior: override channels cleared; one stderr line announces it

invariants: resolve_task_execution is a pure function of (ctx, task, prompt_result, config) — same inputs, same plan
Promotion ctx mutations are applied only after the owning DB transaction commits
userStories.notes: Preserve the deferred-promotion-after-commit contract (CLAUDE.md §'Transactional promotion ctx writes are deferred'). Existing crash-escalation + override-invalidation tests must still pass.
qualityDimensions: Plan equivalence across paths is the convergence proof for #2/#3

Per-slot isolation: a PromptTooLong on one task's invocation does not corrupt another task's overflow_recovered / model_overrides entries

Task id containing `..` → sanitized before filesystem write

acceptanceCriteria: Human-review fires for the same completed requires_human task on both the sequential and wave shapes

Budget: a WaitedAndRetry/RateLimit wave does NOT advance the loop-bound iteration count against max_iterations on either path
Termination: a persistently-rate-limited sequence still exits on signal/.stop (bounded), not unbounded
Known-bad discriminator: a budget helper that only skips the iterations_completed stat (but not the loop-bound iteration) FAILS the no-consumption test

edgeCases: requires_human task completed in a mixed (partially rate-limited) wave → review fires
Persistent rate limit → exits on signal, bounded by MAX_WAIT_SECS

failureModes.cause: Review session spawned while ephemerals unmerged

invariants: iteration_consumed=false ⟺ loop-bound iteration is given back (single shared accounting helper)

userStories.description: Implement reactions::account::react_to_outputs(conn, &[OutputReactionItem], &AccountReactionParams) -> AccountReaction (+ react_to_outputs_inner taking the wait as an injected &dyn Fn). Detect RateLimit across the slice, reset affected in_progress tasks to todo via TaskLifecycle::recover_in_progress_for_prefix, perform the usage wait ONCE (usage::check_and_wait → parse_reset_from_output + wait_for_usage_reset with probe_rate_limit_lifted). Replace iteration.rs:621-688 with a one-item call. Insert the wave call after the process_slot_result loop (wave_scheduler.rs:1024), before handle_task_failure/crash/merge-back. Thread usage_params into WaveIterationParams (engine.rs:548-595) wired from orchestrator.rs:991.
userStories.notes: THE headline bug fix. B3 verified at wave_scheduler.rs:738-739 (empty failed_merges zeroes the streak). B2 verified at orchestrator.rs:918-919/1021-1023/1256. Mixed-wave deferred-merge: a completed-but-unmerged slot from a rate-limited wave should reappear in next wave's ephemeral_overlay — verify; if the early return skips the overlay build, refresh it. Data contract: OutputReactionItem { task_id: Option<&str>, outcome: &IterationOutcome, output: &str } built from SlotResult.iteration_result.{task_id,outcome,output} (filter claim_succeeded).
qualityDimensions: Wait exactly once per wave (account-global)
B1 completion durability before reset (filter status='in_progress')
B2 no max_iterations consumption on WaitedAndRetry (give back loop-bound iteration)
B3 preserve ctx.consecutive_merge_fail_waves on the early return
Stop maps to terminal exit 130 with output:"" (sequential parity)
No unwrap() on the wait/reset paths
userStories.title: react_to_outputs — converge the rate-limit wait (fixes bug #3, audit #6)
